demographic_audit_note: Verified Medicare Advantage PPO status. Cross-checked Atlantis face sheet against facility records; corrected zip code from 19102 to 19104. Confirmed daughter (POA) is primary contact.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Home (SW confirmed discharge to daughter's residence after family meeting).
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: No, never heard of the program. Q2: Thinks we are hospital transport. Q3: No one has explained home health yet. Q4: Very anxious; patient wants home but son is demanding LTC placement due to fall risk. Escalated family conflict to SW.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: PT reports patient is weight-bearing as tolerated with walker. SW reports family meeting held; son agreed to home trial if 24/7 care is arranged for the first week.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into daughter's phone contacts; program flyer delivered to patient and daughter at bedside.
hha_confirmation_status: In Place: Kindred HHA confirmed SOC for 11/14, Nurse 'Elena M.' assigned, first-visit window 11:00 AM - 1:00 PM.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed firm 11/13 D/C date; HHA orders faxed and received. Call 2 (to daughter): Confirmed hospital bed and shower chair are in the home; daughter understands Elena from Kindred arrives tomorrow between 11-1.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached daughter 24 hours prior to D/C; confirmed no changes in health and readiness for morning discharge.
atlantis_final_sync: D/C date 11/13 and 1st visit date 11/14 entered into Atlantis queue.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for 11/14 at 2:30 PM (coordinated to follow HHA morning window).
narrative_summary: 72yo Evelyn's transition was initially stalled by a high-friction disagreement between her son and daughter regarding LTC vs. Home care. The PN identified this barrier during the Stage 1 Connection Intake and escalated it to the SW for a formal family conference. Once the home goal was solidified, the PN executed the Confidence Loop by verifying Kindred HHA was 'In Place' before scheduling the MA. The case resulted in a successful home transition with all stakeholders aligned on the Day 1 plan.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Connection Intake Q4 surfaces son's refusal of home plan.
HHA intake coordinator confirms nurse availability in 19104.
Confidence Audit Call 2: Daughter confirms DME delivery.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient's son is vocally opposing home discharge in the presence of the patient.
format_2_triples.action_taken: PN documented the concern and requested the SW lead a family transition meeting.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA referral sent but intake coordinator has not provided a nurse name.
format_2_triples.action_taken: PN delayed MA scheduling and called HHA intake to confirm 'In Place' status.

format_2_triples.situation: Family is unsure what to do if the HHA nurse does not arrive at the scheduled time.
format_2_triples.action_taken: PN walked daughter through the Day 1 plan and provided the HHA's direct agency line.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Upon hearing the son's demand for LTC, the PN steps back and waits for the SW to update the goal in Atlantis before resuming any family education.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW boundary regarding placement decisions and prevents the PN from overstepping into clinical/social advocacy during a dispute.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN conducts the Connection Intake, identifies the son's anxiety as the primary barrier, escalates the specific conflict to the SW, and performs the Confidence Audit once the goal is cleared.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Follows the 3-stage lifecycle by identifying barriers early and using the SW as the escalation point while maintaining the Confidence Loop.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN provides the son with a cost-comparison analysis showing home care is cheaper than LTC and suggests a specific HHA that specializes in post-hip fracture rehab to win him over.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Violates the Banned Actions by providing cost analysis and suggesting specific HHA agencies, which is the SW's responsibility.
